deep_dive.title: On the Expressive Power of Weight Quantization in Large Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: In recent years, weight quantization that encodes the learnable parameters of large language models in an $n$-bit format has garnered significant attention due to its potential for model compression and inference acceleration. Many practical techniques have been developed; however, the theoretical understanding of many aspects, especially the approximation and degradation of expressive power as the number of quantization bits decreases, remains unclear. In this paper, we provide a theoretical investigation into the expressive capability of large language models relative to the number of quantization bits. We argue that 1.58-bit is the limiting precision for weight quantization by establishing the universal approximation and expressive collapse properties of weight-quantized models with respect to the number of quantization bits. Additionally, we confirm that weight quantization leads to expressive degradation, in which the expressive capacity of weight-quantized models degrades polynomially as the number of quantization bits decreases. These theoretical findings provide a solid foundation for advancing weight quantization in the context of scaling laws and shed insights for future research in model compression and inference acceleration.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: theoretical study of weight quantization in LLMs, directly addressing requirement 3
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对大语言模型权重量化的理论研究，直接满足需求3
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对大语言模型权重量化的理论研究，直接满足需求3
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对大语言模型权重量化的理论研究，直接满足需求3
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper provides a theoretical investigation of weight quantization in LLMs, establishing that 1.58-bit is the limiting precision for expressive power, impacting inference acceleration.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文对大语言模型（LLM）中的权重量化进行了理论探索，证明了1.58比特是维持模型表达能力的极限精度，揭示了量化位数减少带来的表达能力退化规律。该工作为低比特量化的实际部署提供了理论基础，对量化推理加速具有重要指导意义，但与组核优化或MoE场景无直接关联。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文对大语言模型（LLM）中的权重量化进行了理论探索，证明了1.58比特是维持模型表达能力的极限精度，揭示了量化位数减少带来的表达能力退化规律。该工作为低比特量化的实际部署提供了理论基础，对量化推理加速具有重要指导意义，但与组核优化或MoE场景无直接关联。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: weight quantization for large language models

deep_dive.title: Grouped Query Experts: Mixture-of-Experts on GQA Self-Attention
deep_dive.abstract: Self-attention is central to Transformer performance and is often the most expensive part of the Transformer at long context lengths because its pairwise token interactions scale quadratically with sequence length. Standard dense attention also applies the same set of attention heads to every token regardless of token difficulty or information content. This uniform activation can waste compute, especially as sequences grow longer and attention cost increases rapidly. We propose Grouped Query Experts (GQE), a mixture-of-experts layer on top of grouped-query attention (GQA). Within each GQA group, a router selects k query-head experts per token while all key-value (KV) heads remain dense and unchanged. Thus, GQE keeps the KV cache benefits of GQA and reduces only the active query-head computation. On a fixed 30B token budget at the 250M parameter scale, GQE matches the all-active GQA baseline in downstream accuracy while activating half the query heads per token.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Grouped query experts for self-attention optimizing grouped kernel computation in MoE
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自注意力中的分组查询专家优化MoE分组核计算
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自注意力中的分组查询专家优化MoE分组核计算
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自注意力中的分组查询专家优化MoE分组核计算
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: GQE introduces mixture-of-experts on grouped-query attention, selecting query-head experts per token to reduce computation while preserving KV cache benefits, enabling efficient attention for long contexts.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 长序列下自注意力计算成本高昂，且均匀激活所有注意力头造成浪费。本文提出分组查询专家（GQE），在分组查询注意力（GQA）基础上引入混合专家层，每token仅选择k个查询头专家，保持键值头稠密。该方法在保持注意力质量的同时显著降低计算量，并继承GQA的KV缓存优势，为高效Transformer推理提供了新方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 长序列下自注意力计算成本高昂，且均匀激活所有注意力头造成浪费。本文提出分组查询专家（GQE），在分组查询注意力（GQA）基础上引入混合专家层，每token仅选择k个查询头专家，保持键值头稠密。该方法在保持注意力质量的同时显著降低计算量，并继承GQA的KV缓存优势，为高效Transformer推理提供了新方案。

deep_dive.title: Does Mixture-of-Experts Actually Help Inference on Consumer and Edge Hardware? An Empirical Study
deep_dive.abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models are often described as ideal for resource-constrained inference. Each token activates only a small subset of experts, so the per-token compute cost, in floating-point operations (FLOPs), resembles that of a much smaller dense model. Whether that FLOP advantage survives in practice is far less clear. We ask whether MoE models actually run faster and cheaper than comparable dense models on consumer-grade and edge hardware. We benchmark OLMoE-1B-7B (1.3 B active of 6.9 B total) against three dense baselines on an Apple M2 Pro and an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano 8 GB through llama.cpp, measuring throughput, memory, and on-device energy. The answer is device-dependent: OLMoE's active-parameter advantage is only partly realised on the laptop (~10% behind the same-active Llama-3.2-1B) and erodes on the edge device (~31% behind, at 2.1$\times$ the energy per token, with peak memory at the 8 GB ceiling). Patching llama.cpp to time the decode graph node-by-node shows routing accounts for under 9% of MoE-block compute on the cleaner edge backend, so the gap reflects total-parameter memory footprint, expert dispatch, and KV-cache pressure rather than routing. The implication is that on bandwidth-bound edge hardware, inference cost tracks total parameters, not active ones, and sparse activation does not buy back what the device is constrained on. These findings are bounded to one MoE model at this parameter scale and two devices, and we release the full measurement harness and per-run data.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: benchmarks MoE inference on edge hardware
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 在消费级和边缘硬件上基准测试MoE推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 在消费级和边缘硬件上基准测试MoE推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 在消费级和边缘硬件上基准测试MoE推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Empirical study benchmarks MoE model (OLMoE-1B-7B) against dense baselines on consumer/edge hardware, finding device-dependent performance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文实证研究了MoE模型在消费级和边缘硬件上的推理效率。通过基准测试OLMoE-1B-7B与三个稠密基线模型，发现MoE的性能优势依赖于具体设备，为高效MoE推理提供了关键实验参考。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文实证研究了MoE模型在消费级和边缘硬件上的推理效率。通过基准测试OLMoE-1B-7B与三个稠密基线模型，发现MoE的性能优势依赖于具体设备，为高效MoE推理提供了关键实验参考。

deep_dive.title: ASAP: A Disaggregated and Asynchronous Inference System for MoE Prefill
deep_dive.abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models have become the de facto standard for scaling large language models. To maintain computational efficiency, modern MoE serving systems typically employ a hybrid parallelism strategy, combining Data Parallelism (DP) for attention stages with Expert Parallelism (EP) for MoE stages. However, this design necessitates frequent global synchronization barriers between attention DP groups and experts. In online serving, significant variance in request arrival rates and sequence lengths inherently leads to DP imbalance, causing severe synchronization stalls that degrade Time-to-First-Token (TTFT) and system throughput. We present ASAP, an asynchronous inference system specifically designed to accelerate the prefill phase of MoE models. ASAP disaggregates the attention and MoE stages and implements a fully asynchronous execution pipeline. This is achieved through a suite of specialized asynchronous communication primitives and four coordinated optimizations across request scheduling and model execution, which collectively dismantle global synchronization barriers. We implement and evaluate ASAP on CloudMatrix384 super-nodes, demonstrating that it improves SLO-compliant prefill throughput by 90% compared to state-of-the-art synchronous serving solutions.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Asynchronous inference system for MoE prefill stage
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于MoE预填充阶段的异步推理系统
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于MoE预填充阶段的异步推理系统
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于MoE预填充阶段的异步推理系统
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ASAP disaggregates and asynchronizes MoE model prefill inference, decoupling attention DP and expert EP stages to eliminate synchronization barriers, improving TTFT and throughput under realistic request variability.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有MoE服务系统在混合并行中存在频繁同步屏障，请求不平衡导致严重性能下降。本文提出ASAP，将预填充阶段解耦为异步执行，解除注意力数据并行与专家并行之间的同步依赖。实验表明，ASAP显著降低了首Token延迟并提升了系统吞吐量，为MoE在线推理提供了高效架构。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有MoE服务系统在混合并行中存在频繁同步屏障，请求不平衡导致严重性能下降。本文提出ASAP，将预填充阶段解耦为异步执行，解除注意力数据并行与专家并行之间的同步依赖。实验表明，ASAP显著降低了首Token延迟并提升了系统吞吐量，为MoE在线推理提供了高效架构。

quick_skim.title: Fast-TurboQuant: A Multiplier-Free Online Vector Quantization Approach
quick_skim.abstract: As large language models scale, memory bandwidth for key-value caches and retrieval-augmented generation systems becomes a critical bottleneck. While 1-bit quantization addresses this constraint, recent TurboQuant relies on dense random rotation matrices to condition the vector distribution before quantization. This projection demands millions of floating-point multiplications per embedding, making it difficult to deploy on constrained edge silicon. We introduce Fast-TurboQuant, a multiplier-free projection architecture that replaces the dense matrix with a structured fast Johnson-Lindenstrauss transform. By applying a Rademacher phase inversion followed by a fast Walsh-Hadamard transform (FWHT), the method leverages sub-Gaussian concentration to satisfy the prerequisites of scalar Lloyd-Max quantization without Gaussian projections. This substitution reduces the arithmetic complexity to only additions, eliminating hardware multipliers. Evaluation on DBpedia OpenAI-3 Large embeddings demonstrates a 19.7 times algorithmic speedup under sequential execution. Furthermore, the dimension expansion due to the FWHT zero-padding reduces the mean squared error and improves Recall@10.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: quantization for LLMs with fast transforms
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对LLM的快速变换量化方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对LLM的快速变换量化方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对LLM的快速变换量化方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Fast-TurboQuant introduces a multiplier-free online vector quantization using fast Walsh-Hadamard transform to reduce computations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出Fast-TurboQuant，一种免乘法在线向量量化方法。通过快速Walsh-Hadamard变换替代密集旋转矩阵，在保持量化质量的同时大幅降低计算量，适用于资源受限的LLM部署。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出Fast-TurboQuant，一种免乘法在线向量量化方法。通过快速Walsh-Hadamard变换替代密集旋转矩阵，在保持量化质量的同时大幅降低计算量，适用于资源受限的LLM部署。
